body_txt:
What accessory could you possibly add to up your sex appeal 20 notches, show your sensitive side, and look really cool ? A rose, of course! It's a flower, so it's beautiful and romantic like you, but it has thorns, and you're no wuss. Hold it between your teeth, if you want to up your sex appeal a few more notches. Perfect!
And in some cases, it can be weaponized into a whip , a dart, and/or petal storm ! Generally, this is used to indicate a character is a Pretty Boy , a girly and badass man , or a Casanova . Princely types like an actual Prince Charming or a Princely Young Man are also often given rose motifs. That said, there are a few female examples out there, like The Vamp , the Girly Bruiser , and the Bifauxnen , which intentionally invokes Bishōnen imagery. Sometimes, the color, number, and position of the roses can be used for Foreshadowing . This usually happens in reference to the Victorian custom of "the language of flowers," in which different flowers were given different meanings, allowing a bouquet to transmit a sort of informal coded message. This same language of flowers helped give rise to modern Floral Motifs . Speaking of Victorians, the common depictions of roses we see today are a relatively recent phenomenon . Commonly used depictions use a type of cultivar known as the hybrid tea rose , which was first bred in the late Victorian era, and is probably the most famous rose cultivar in the present day, being instantly recognisable thanks to its spiral petal arrangement. Earlier depictions such as in medieval illuminated manuscripts and in the emblems of the Houses of Lancaster and York used non-spiral, five-petal roses; some of which then are often colored pink (which is the oldest and most common color that's cultivated) or in cooler hues of red, by which other languages such as German, Russian and all major Romance languages use "rose" as a stand-in for pink . The early cultivars were given more significance due having stronger fragrances at certain points of the year, which is most sought after in making rosewater , while the hybrid tea has a faint scent. In Japan, gay and bisexual men are referred to as barazoku ("rose tribe"), so roses in Japanese media can also mean an entirely different kind of coded message . Related to Flowers of Femininity and Flowers of Romance . Compare and contrast The Tragic Rose . Sub-Trope of Red Is Romantic , with which it may overlap.
